lede: A look at December 14 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Francisco Conceição and Chris Brunt, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting
events.desc: Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting: Twenty-eight people, including the gunman, are killed in Sandy Hook, Connecticut.

events.title: Vargas tragedy
events.desc: Torrential rains cause flash floods in Vargas, Venezuela, resulting in tens of thousands of deaths, the destruction of thousands of homes, and the complete collapse of the state's infrastructure.

events.title: War in Abkhazia (1992–1993)
events.desc: War in Abkhazia: Siege of Tkvarcheli: A helicopter carrying evacuees from Tkvarcheli is shot down, resulting in at least 52 deaths, including 25 children. The incident catalyses more concerted Russian military intervention on behalf of Abkhazia.

events.title: Thomas T. Goldsmith Jr.
events.desc: Thomas T. Goldsmith Jr. and Estle Ray Mann are granted a patent for their cathode-ray tube amusement device, the earliest known interactive electronic game.

events.title: Wozzeck
events.desc: Wozzeck, Alban Berg's first opera, is premiered at the Berlin State Opera conducted by Erich Kleiber.

events.title: Giacomo Puccini
events.desc: Giacomo Puccini's comic opera Gianni Schicchi premieres at the Metropolitan Opera in New York City.

events.title: Japanese battleship Haruna
events.desc: Haruna, the fourth and last Kongō-class ship, launches, eventually becoming one of the Japanese workhorses during World War I and World War II.

events.title: Roald Amundsen
events.desc: Roald Amundsen's team, comprising himself, Olav Bjaaland, Helmer Hanssen, Sverre Hassel, and Oscar Wisting, becomes the first to reach the South Pole.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The Confederate victory under General James Longstreet at the Battle of Bean's Station in East Tennessee ends the Knoxville Campaign, but achieves very little as Longstreet returns to Virginia next spring.

events.title: Toledo War
events.desc: The Toledo War unofficially ends as the "Frostbitten Convention" votes to accept Congress' terms for admitting Michigan as a U.S. state.

births.name: Helle Thorning-Schmidt
births.line: Danish academic and politician, 41st Prime Minister of Denmark

deaths.text: Edward Jones (North Carolina politician), American police officer and politician
G. C. Edmondson, American soldier and author
Marjorie Kinnan Rawlings, American author and academic

observances.text: Christian feast day: John of the Cross
Christian feast day: John III of the Sedre (Syriac Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Blessed Mary Frances Schervier
